data.narrative:
Skydio, a Series E autonomous systems company in Defense & Security, is valued at $2.2B post-money following $230M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis triangulates against a universe of 10 comparables: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Flock Safety, Saronic, Anduril Industries, and Shield AI.

Two methods anchor the valuation range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x multiplier typical of Series E-to-later-stage advancement, implying $3.1B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Flock Safety ($7.5B Series E), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), and Epirus ($1.4B Series D)—yields a $6.0B median, with a full range of $1.4B to $9.2B.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $2.6B (low) to $6.9B (high), with a median of $4.5B. The current $2.2B valuation sits below the low end of this range, suggesting either a conservative mark or material execution risk priced into the last financing. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to better-capitalized peers and pathway to sustainable defense-sector adoption. The wide peer dispersion ($1.4B–$9.2B) reflects heterogeneity in business model, market exposure, and maturity within the sector.
